lines.lat: In nómine Patris, et Fílii, et Spíritus Sancti.
lines.eng: In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.

lines.lat: Amen.
lines.eng: Amen.

lines.lat: Pater noster, qui es in cælis, sanctificétur nomen tuum.
lines.eng: Our Father, Who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name.

lines.lat: Advéniat regnum tuum. Fiat volúntas tua, sicut in cælo et in terra.
lines.eng: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

lines.lat: Panem nostrum quotidiánum da nobis hódie,
lines.eng: Give us this day our daily bread,

lines.lat: et dimítte nobis débita nostra, sicut et nos dimíttimus debitóribus nostris.
lines.eng: and forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us.

lines.lat: Et ne nos indúcas in tentatiónem: sed líbera nos a malo.
lines.eng: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.

lines.lat: ℟.Amen.
lines.eng: ℟.Amen.

lines.lat: Ave María, grátia plena, Dóminus tecum.
lines.eng: Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee.

lines.lat: Benedícta tu in muliéribus, et benedíctus fructus ventris tui, Iesus.
lines.eng: Blessed art thou amongst women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb, Jesus.

lines.lat: ℟.Sancta María, Mater Dei, ora pro nobis peccatóribus,
lines.eng: ℟.Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us sinners,

lines.lat: nunc et in hora mortis nostræ. Amen.
lines.eng: now and at the hour of our death. Amen.

lines.lat: Glória Patri, et Fílio, et Spirítui Sancto.
lines.eng: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Ghost.

lines.lat: Sicut erat in princípio, et nunc, et semper,
lines.eng: As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be,

lines.lat: et in sǽcula sæculórum. Amen.
lines.eng: world without end. Amen.

title: Oratio Fátimæ
note: Added after each Gloria Patri in the Rosary, as revealed by Our Lady at Fatima in 1917.

lines.lat: O mi Iesu, dimítte nobis débita nostra,
lines.eng: O my Jesus, forgive us our sins,

lines.lat: líbera nos ab igne inférni,
lines.eng: save us from the fires of hell,

lines.lat: perduc in cælum omnes ánimas,
lines.eng: lead all souls to Heaven,

lines.lat: præsértim eas, quæ misericórdiæ tuæ máxime índigent. Amen.
lines.eng: especially those most in need of Thy mercy. Amen.

title: Salve Regína
note: Said at the end of the Rosary, and as the Marian antiphon of Compline from Trinity Sunday through Advent.

lines.lat: Salve, Regína, Mater misericórdiæ,
lines.eng: Hail, holy Queen, Mother of Mercy,

lines.lat: vita, dulcédo et spes nostra, salve.
lines.eng: our life, our sweetness, and our hope, hail.

lines.lat: Ad te clamámus, éxsules fílii Hevæ.
lines.eng: To thee do we cry, poor banished children of Eve.

lines.lat: Ad te suspirámus, geméntes et flentes in hac lacrimárum valle.
lines.eng: To thee do we send up our sighs, mourning and weeping in this valley of tears.

lines.lat: Eia ergo, advocáta nostra, illos tuos misericórdes óculos ad nos convérte.
lines.eng: Turn then, most gracious Advocate, thine eyes of mercy toward us.

lines.lat: Et Iesum, benedíctum fructum ventris tui, nobis post hoc exsílium osténde.
lines.eng: And after this our exile, show unto us the blessed fruit of thy womb, Jesus.

lines.lat: O clemens, o pia, o dulcis Virgo María. Amen.
lines.eng: O clement, O loving, O sweet Virgin Mary. Amen.

title: Credo Apostolórum
note: Said at the beginning of the Rosary, holding the crucifix.

lines.lat: Credo in Deum Patrem omnipoténtem, Creatórem cæli et terræ.
lines.eng: I believe in God, the Father Almighty, Creator of heaven and earth.

lines.lat: Et in Iesum Christum, Fílium eius únicum, Dóminum nostrum:
lines.eng: And in Jesus Christ, His only Son, our Lord:

lines.lat: qui concéptus est de Spíritu Sancto, natus ex María Vírgine,
lines.eng: Who was conceived by the Holy Ghost, born of the Virgin Mary,

lines.lat: passus sub Póntio Piláto, crucifíxus, mórtuus, et sepúltus:
lines.eng: suffered under Pontius Pilate, was crucified, died, and was buried.

lines.lat: descéndit ad ínferos; tértia die resurréxit a mórtuis;
lines.eng: He descended into hell; the third day He rose again from the dead;

lines.lat: ascéndit ad cælos; sedet ad déxteram Dei Patris omnipoténtis:
lines.eng: He ascended into heaven, and sitteth at the right hand of God the Father Almighty:

lines.lat: inde ventúrus est iudicáre vivos et mórtuos.
lines.eng: from thence He shall come to judge the living and the dead.

lines.lat: Credo in Spíritum Sanctum, sanctam Ecclésiam cathólicam,
lines.eng: I believe in the Holy Ghost, the Holy Catholic Church,

lines.lat: Sanctórum communiónem, remissiónem peccatórum,
lines.eng: the communion of Saints, the forgiveness of sins,

lines.lat: carnis resurrectiónem, vitam ætérnam. Amen.
lines.eng: the resurrection of the body, and life everlasting. Amen.

title: Confíteor
note: Said at the foot of the altar at the beginning of Mass, and at Compline.

lines.lat: Confíteor Deo omnipoténti, beátæ Maríæ semper Vírgini,
lines.eng: I confess to almighty God, to blessed Mary ever Virgin,

lines.lat: beáto Michaéli Archángelo, beáto Ioánni Baptístæ,
lines.eng: to blessed Michael the Archangel, to blessed John the Baptist,

lines.lat: sanctis Apóstolis Petro et Paulo, et ómnibus Sanctis,
lines.eng: to the holy Apostles Peter and Paul, and to all the Saints,

lines.lat: quia peccávi nimis cogitatióne, verbo et ópere:
lines.eng: that I have sinned exceedingly in thought, word, and deed:

lines.lat: mea culpa, mea culpa, mea máxima culpa.
lines.eng: through my fault, through my fault, through my most grievous fault.

lines.lat: Ideo precor beátam Maríam semper Vírginem, beátum Michaélem Archángelum,
lines.eng: Therefore I beseech blessed Mary ever Virgin, blessed Michael the Archangel,

lines.lat: beátum Ioánnem Baptístam, sanctos Apóstolos Petrum et Paulum, et omnes Sanctos,
lines.eng: blessed John the Baptist, the holy Apostles Peter and Paul, and all the Saints,

lines.lat: oráre pro me ad Dóminum Deum nostrum. Amen.
lines.eng: to pray for me to the Lord our God. Amen.

title: Kýrie Eléison
note: Said in three groups of three at the beginning of Mass.

lines.lat: Kýrie, eléison.
lines.eng: Lord, have mercy.

lines.lat: Kýrie, eléison.
lines.eng: Lord, have mercy.

lines.lat: Kýrie, eléison.
lines.eng: Lord, have mercy.

lines.lat: Christe, eléison.
lines.eng: Christ, have mercy.

lines.lat: Christe, eléison.
lines.eng: Christ, have mercy.

lines.lat: Christe, eléison.
lines.eng: Christ, have mercy.

lines.lat: Kýrie, eléison.
lines.eng: Lord, have mercy.

lines.lat: Kýrie, eléison.
lines.eng: Lord, have mercy.

lines.lat: Kýrie, eléison.
lines.eng: Lord, have mercy.

title: Glória in Excélsis Deo
eng: Glory to God in the Highest
note: The Greater Doxology. Sung at Mass on Sundays (outside Advent and Lent), feasts, and within octaves.

lines.lat: Glória in excélsis Deo, et in terra pax homínibus bonæ voluntátis.
lines.eng: Glory be to God on high, and on earth peace to men of good will.

lines.lat: Laudámus te. Benedícimus te. Adorámus te. Glorificámus te.
lines.eng: We praise Thee. We bless Thee. We adore Thee. We glorify Thee.

lines.lat: Grátias ágimus tibi propter magnam glóriam tuam.
lines.eng: We give Thee thanks for Thy great glory.

lines.lat: Dómine Deus, Rex cæléstis, Deus Pater omnípotens.
lines.eng: O Lord God, heavenly King, God the Father almighty.

lines.lat: Dómine Fili Unigénite, Iesu Christe. Dómine Deus, Agnus Dei, Fílius Patris.
lines.eng: O Lord Jesus Christ, the only-begotten Son. O Lord God, Lamb of God, Son of the Father.

lines.lat: Qui tollis peccáta mundi, miserére nobis.
lines.eng: Thou Who takest away the sins of the world, have mercy on us.

lines.lat: Qui tollis peccáta mundi, súscipe deprecatiónem nostram.
lines.eng: Thou Who takest away the sins of the world, receive our prayer.

lines.lat: Qui sedes ad déxteram Patris, miserére nobis.
lines.eng: Thou Who sittest at the right hand of the Father, have mercy on us.

lines.lat: Quóniam tu solus Sanctus. Tu solus Dóminus. Tu solus Altíssimus, Iesu Christe.
lines.eng: For Thou alone art holy. Thou alone art the Lord. Thou alone art the most high, Jesus Christ.

lines.lat: Cum Sancto Spíritu, in glória Dei Patris. Amen.
lines.eng: With the Holy Ghost, in the glory of God the Father. Amen.

title: Sanctus
note: Sung at every Mass before the Canon. All kneel.

lines.lat: Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus, Dóminus Deus Sábaoth.
lines.eng: Holy, Holy, Holy, Lord God of Hosts.

lines.lat: Pleni sunt cæli et terra glória tua.
lines.eng: Heaven and earth are full of Thy glory.

lines.lat: Hosánna in excélsis.
lines.eng: Hosanna in the highest.

lines.lat: ✠ Benedíctus qui venit in nómine Dómini.
lines.eng: ✠ Blessed is He Who cometh in the name of the Lord.

lines.lat: Hosánna in excélsis.
lines.eng: Hosanna in the highest.

lines.lat: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccáta mundi: miserére nobis.
lines.eng: Lamb of God, Who takest away the sins of the world: have mercy on us.

lines.lat: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccáta mundi: miserére nobis.
lines.eng: Lamb of God, Who takest away the sins of the world: have mercy on us.

lines.lat: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccáta mundi: dona nobis pacem.
lines.eng: Lamb of God, Who takest away the sins of the world: grant us peace.

title: Dómine, Non Sum Dignus
note: Said three times before receiving Holy Communion, striking the breast at each repetition.

lines.lat: Dómine, non sum dignus, ut intres sub tectum meum:
lines.eng: Lord, I am not worthy that Thou shouldst enter under my roof:

lines.lat: sed tantum dic verbo, et sanábitur ánima mea.
lines.eng: but only say the word, and my soul shall be healed.

lines.lat: (Said three times.)
lines.eng: (Said three times.)

title: Oblátio Matutína
note: Traditionally said upon rising, to consecrate the day's prayers, works, joys, and sufferings to the Sacred Heart of Jesus through the Immaculate Heart of Mary.

lines.lat: O Iesu, per Cor Immaculátum Maríæ,
lines.eng: O Jesus, through the Immaculate Heart of Mary,

lines.lat: óffero tibi oratiónes, ópera, gáudia et labóres huius diéi
lines.eng: I offer Thee the prayers, works, joys, and sufferings of this day

lines.lat: in satisfactiónem pro peccátis nostris,
lines.eng: in satisfaction for our sins,

lines.lat: in ómnibus Missis Sacrificiis hódie per orbem celebrátis,
lines.eng: in union with all the Holy Sacrifices of the Mass celebrated throughout the world today,

lines.lat: pro ómnibus intentiónibus Sacratíssimi Cordis tui,
lines.eng: for all the intentions of Thy Most Sacred Heart,

lines.lat: pro unióne christianórum, pro conversióne peccatórum,
lines.eng: for the reunion of all Christians, for the conversion of sinners,

lines.lat: ac pro intentiónibus Summi Pontíficis hoc mense. Amen.
lines.eng: and for the intentions of our Holy Father this month. Amen.

title: Memoráre
note: A prayer to Our Lady by St. Bernard of Clairvaux (12th century).

lines.lat: Memoráre, O piíssima Virgo María, non esse audítum a sǽculo,
lines.eng: Remember, O most gracious Virgin Mary, that never was it known

lines.lat: quemquam ad tua curréntem præsídia, tua implorántem auxília, tua peténtem suffrágia,
lines.eng: that anyone who fled to thy protection, implored thy help, or sought thine intercession,

lines.lat: esse derelíctum.
lines.eng: was left unaided.

lines.lat: Ego tali animátus confidéntia, ad te, Virgo Vírginum, Mater, curro,
lines.eng: Inspired by this confidence, I fly unto thee, O Virgin of virgins, my Mother;

lines.lat: ad te vénio, coram te gemens peccátor assísto.
lines.eng: to thee do I come, before thee I stand, sinful and sorrowful.

lines.lat: Noli, Mater Verbi, verba mea despícere,
lines.eng: O Mother of the Word Incarnate, despise not my petitions,

lines.lat: sed áudi propítia et exáudi. Amen.
lines.eng: but in thy mercy hear and answer me. Amen.

title: Actus Contritiónis
note: The traditional Latin Act of Contrition, distinct from the English form ('O my God, I am heartily sorry…') but equally valid. Said daily and in the Sacrament of Penance.

lines.lat: Deus meus, ex toto corde pǽnitet me ómnium meórum peccatórum,
lines.eng: My God, with my whole heart I repent of all my sins,

lines.lat: éaque detéstor, quia peccándo,
lines.eng: and I detest them, because by sinning

lines.lat: non solum pœnas a te iuste statútas proméritus sum,
lines.eng: I have not only deserved the punishments justly decreed by Thee,

lines.lat: sed præsértim quia offéndi te, summum bonum, ac dignum qui super ómnia diligáris.
lines.eng: but above all because I have offended Thee, the supreme good, worthy to be loved above all things.

lines.lat: Ideo fírmiter propóno, adiuvánte grátia tua,
lines.eng: Therefore I firmly resolve, with the help of Thy grace,

lines.lat: de cétero me non peccatúrum peccandíque occasiónes próximas fugitúrum. Amen.
lines.eng: henceforth to sin no more and to flee the near occasions of sin. Amen.

title: Ánima Christi
note: A prayer of thanksgiving after Holy Communion, often attributed to St. Ignatius of Loyola.

lines.lat: Ánima Christi, sanctífica me.
lines.eng: Soul of Christ, sanctify me.

lines.lat: Corpus Christi, salva me.
lines.eng: Body of Christ, save me.

lines.lat: Sanguis Christi, inébria me.
lines.eng: Blood of Christ, inebriate me.

lines.lat: Aqua láteris Christi, lava me.
lines.eng: Water from the side of Christ, wash me.

lines.lat: Pássio Christi, confórta me.
lines.eng: Passion of Christ, strengthen me.

lines.lat: O bone Iesu, exáudi me.
lines.eng: O good Jesus, hear me.

lines.lat: Intra tua vúlnera abscónde me.
lines.eng: Within Thy wounds hide me.

lines.lat: Ne permíttas me separári a te.
lines.eng: Permit me not to be separated from Thee.

lines.lat: Ab hoste malígno defénde me.
lines.eng: From the malignant enemy defend me.

lines.lat: In hora mortis meæ voca me.
lines.eng: In the hour of my death call me.

lines.lat: Et iube me veníre ad te,
lines.eng: And bid me come unto Thee,

lines.lat: ut cum Sanctis tuis laudem te,
lines.eng: that with Thy saints I may praise Thee,

lines.lat: ℟.in sǽcula sæculórum. Amen.
lines.eng: ℟.for ever and ever. Amen.

title: En Ego, O Bone Iesu
note: To be said after Communion, kneeling before a crucifix. A plenary indulgence may be gained on any Friday in Lent.

lines.lat: En ego, o bone et dulcíssime Iesu, ante conspéctum tuum génibus me provólvo,
lines.eng: Behold, O good and most sweet Jesus, I cast myself upon my knees in Thy sight,

lines.lat: ac máximo ánimi ardóre te oro atque obtéstor,
lines.eng: and with the most fervent desire of my soul I pray and beseech Thee

lines.lat: ut meum in cor vívidos fídei, spei et caritátis sensus,
lines.eng: that Thou wouldst impress upon my heart lively sentiments of faith, hope, and charity,

lines.lat: atque veram peccatórum meórum pæniténtiam,
lines.eng: with true contrition for my sins,

lines.lat: éaque emendándi firmíssimam voluntátem velis imprímere;
lines.eng: and a firm purpose of amendment;

lines.lat: dum magno ánimi afféctu et dolóre tua quinque vúlnera mecum ipse consídero,
lines.eng: while with deep affection and grief of soul I ponder within myself and mentally contemplate Thy five most precious wounds,

lines.lat: illud præ óculis habens, quod iam in ore ponébat tuo David Prophéta de te, o bone Iesu:
lines.eng: having before my eyes that which Thy prophet David spake concerning Thee, O good Jesus:

lines.lat: Fodérunt manus meas et pedes meos: dinumeravérunt ómnia ossa mea. (Ps 21:17–18)
lines.eng: They have pierced my hands and my feet; they have numbered all my bones. (Ps 21:17–18)

lines.lat: ℟.Amen.
lines.eng: ℟.Amen.

title: Tantum Ergo Sacraméntum
note: The last two stanzas of the Pange Lingua by St. Thomas Aquinas. Sung at Benediction of the Blessed Sacrament.

lines.lat: Tantum ergo Sacraméntum venerémur cérnui:
lines.eng: Therefore we, before Him bending, this great Sacrament revere:

lines.lat: et antíquum documéntum novo cedat rítui:
lines.eng: types and shadows have their ending, for the newer rite is here.

lines.lat: præstet fides suppleméntum sénsuum deféctui.
lines.eng: Faith, our outward sense befriending, makes our inward vision clear.

lines.lat: Genitóri, Genitóque laus et iubilátio,
lines.eng: Glory let us give, and blessing, to the Father and the Son;

lines.lat: salus, honor, virtus quoque sit et benedíctio:
lines.eng: honour, might, and praise addressing, while eternal ages run;

lines.lat: procedénti ab utróque compar sit laudátio. Amen.
lines.eng: ever too His love confessing, Who from both with both is One. Amen.

lines.lat: ℣. Panem de cælo præstitísti eis.
lines.eng: ℣. Thou hast given them bread from Heaven.

lines.lat: ℟. Omne delectaméntum in se habéntem.
lines.eng: ℟. Containing in itself all sweetness.

title: Oratio ad Sanctum Michaélem
eng: Prayer to St. Michael the Archangel
note: Composed by Pope Leo XIII in 1886 after a vision of the spiritual warfare facing the Church. Said after Low Mass until 1965, and widely revived today.

lines.lat: Sancte Míchaël Archángele, defénde nos in prǽlio;
lines.eng: St. Michael the Archangel, defend us in battle;

lines.lat: contra nequítiam et insídias diáboli esto præsídium.
lines.eng: be our protection against the wickedness and snares of the devil.

lines.lat: Ímperet illi Deus, súpplices deprecámur:
lines.eng: May God rebuke him, we humbly pray:

lines.lat: tuque, Princeps milítiæ cæléstis,
lines.eng: and do thou, O Prince of the heavenly host,

lines.lat: Sátanam aliósque spíritus malígnos,
lines.eng: by the power of God cast into hell Satan

lines.lat: qui ad perditiónem animárum pervagántur in mundo,
lines.eng: and all the evil spirits who prowl about the world

lines.lat: divína virtúte, in inférnum detrúde. Amen.
lines.eng: seeking the ruin of souls. Amen.

title: Adóro Te Devóte
note: A Eucharistic hymn by St. Thomas Aquinas, said in thanksgiving after Communion.

lines.lat: Adóro te devóte, latens Déitas,
lines.eng: I devoutly adore Thee, O hidden God,

lines.lat: quæ sub his figúris vere látitas:
lines.eng: truly hidden beneath these appearances:

lines.lat: tibi se cor meum totum súbiicit,
lines.eng: My whole heart submits to Thee,

lines.lat: quia te contémplans totum déficit.
lines.eng: and in contemplating Thee, it surrenders itself completely.

lines.lat: Visus, tactus, gustus in te fállitur,
lines.eng: Sight, touch, taste are all deceived in their judgment of Thee,

lines.lat: sed audítu solo tuto créditur:
lines.eng: but hearing suffices firmly to believe:

lines.lat: credo quidquid dixit Dei Fílius;
lines.eng: I believe all that the Son of God has spoken;

lines.lat: nil hoc verbo veritátis vérius.
lines.eng: there is nothing truer than this Word of Truth.

lines.lat: In cruce latébat sola Déitas,
lines.eng: On the cross only the Divinity was hidden,

lines.lat: at hic latet simul et humánitas:
lines.eng: but here the Humanity is also concealed:

lines.lat: ambo tamen credens atque cónfitens,
lines.eng: yet believing and confessing both,

lines.lat: peto quod petívit latro pǽnitens. Amen.
lines.eng: I ask for what the penitent thief asked. Amen.
